sections.tldr: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA at a glance: UGC-DEB approved, NAAC A, Not listed in NIRF 2025, 2 years (4 semesters, max 4-year validity), Rs 60,000 to Rs 2,00,000 fee range (indicative), 6 specialisations: Finance, Marketing Management, Human Resource Management, Operations Management, Project Management, Logistics and Supply Chain Management. Unique: Startup Management as a core Semester 3 subject. Best for working professionals in Northeast India and East India seeking an affordable UGC-DEB approved online MBA with practical management tracks.

hero.headline: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA — NAAC A, 6 Specialisations, Startup Management in Core
hero.body: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA is UGC-DEB approved and NAAC A accredited, delivered through the university's Centre for Distance and Open Education (CDOE). The 2-year programme offers 6 MBA specialisations: Finance, Marketing Management, Human Resource Management, Operations Management, Project Management, and Logistics and Supply Chain Management. Startup Management appears as a dedicated core subject in Semester 3 — a differentiator rarely found in online MBA programmes at this price point.
hero.sub: NAAC A · Not in NIRF 2025 top lists · UGC-DEB · 6 specialisations · Startup Management in Sem 3 core

about.heading: About Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA
about.body: Assam Don Bosco University (ADBU), located in Guwahati, Assam, is a UGC-recognised private university established by the Salesian Society. It holds NAAC A accreditation and Assam Don Bosco University is not listed in NIRF 2025 rankings. EdifyEdu verifies these accreditation figures independently and does not receive commission from this university. The online MBA is delivered through ADBU's Centre for Distance and Open Education (CDOE) under UGC-DEB entitlement. Six specialisations are available: Finance Management, Marketing Management, Human Resource Management, Operations Management, Project Management, and Logistics and Supply Chain Management. The programme fee ranges from Rs 60,000 to Rs 2,00,000 depending on specialisation and payment plan. EMI starts from Rs 2,500 per month. The maximum programme validity is 4 years from enrolment.

approvals.heading: UGC-DEB Approval and Accreditations
approvals.body: Assam Don Bosco University holds NAAC A accreditation (naac.gov.in) and NIRF not listed in 2025 rankings (nirfindia.org). The online MBA is delivered under UGC-DEB entitlement (deb.ugc.ac.in), making it valid for government jobs, PSU recruitment, and higher education admissions across India. Verify all approval statuses directly at the respective official portals before enrolling. Accreditation grades and UGC-DEB entitlements are reviewed annually; always confirm current status for your admission year before paying any fees.

ugcDeb.heading: UGC-DEB Approval and Degree Validity
ugcDeb.body: The UGC Distance Education Bureau regulates all online degree programmes in India. Assam Don Bosco University appears on the UGC-DEB entitled institutions list at deb.ugc.ac.in. This entitlement means: the degree is valid for government job applications at central and state levels, graduates can pursue higher education at recognised Indian universities, and PSU recruitment boards accept the degree under current UGC norms. The maximum programme duration is 4 years, giving working students flexibility to complete at their own pace within the validity window. UGC-DEB entitlement is reviewed periodically. Verify the approved list at deb.ugc.ac.in for your specific admission year before enrolling.

whoCanApply.heading: Who Can Apply
whoCanApply.body: The minimum eligibility for the Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA is graduation with 50% marks from a recognised university. No age limit applies under UGC-DEB guidelines. Work experience is not mandatory, though the Operations Management, Project Management, and Logistics and Supply Chain Management tracks benefit significantly from prior industry exposure in those sectors. No entrance exam is required for admission. SC/ST and OBC candidates should confirm relaxation norms with the admissions team. Final-year graduates may apply provisionally. Create your Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) ID at abc.gov.in before applying to speed up enrolment.

classes.heading: Class Schedule and Delivery
classes.body: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA is delivered through the ADBU CDOE online learning platform. Recorded lectures, study materials, and assignment briefs are accessible at any time from the semester start. Live sessions are scheduled on weekends and weekday evenings to accommodate working professionals across Northeast India and beyond. The Startup Management subject in Semester 3 uses applied case studies and project-based learning alongside recorded content. The Minor Project in Semester 3 and Research Project or Dissertation in Semester 4 run on structured timelines with faculty supervision. Assignment windows are fixed per semester, with flexible daily scheduling within each window.

exams.heading: Examination Mode
exams.body: All examinations for the Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA are conducted online in a proctored format. A functioning webcam, valid government-issued photo ID, and a stable internet connection are required for each exam session. Continuous assessment through assignments and quizzes contributes 30-40% of the final grade; semester-end proctored examinations contribute 60-70%. The Minor Project in Semester 3 and the Research Project or Dissertation in Semester 4 are separately assessed components. Keep Aadhaar as your primary ID for exam sessions. All 6 specialisations follow the same examination structure.

specializationsOverview.heading: MBA Specialisations at Assam Don Bosco University
specializationsOverview.body: Assam Don Bosco University Online offers 6 MBA specialisations: Finance Management, Marketing Management, Human Resource Management, Operations Management, Project Management, and Logistics and Supply Chain Management. Semesters 1 and 2 cover a shared core curriculum across all tracks. Specialisation-specific subjects begin in Semester 3. The Finance, HRM, and Marketing Management tracks have fully documented 4-semester syllabi. Operations Management, Project Management, and Logistics and Supply Chain Management Semester 3-4 elective lists are being finalised — contact our counsellor or the university for the current subject list for these tracks. Confirm your chosen specialisation is active for your intake before paying any fees.

syllabusNote.heading: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA — Semester-wise Syllabus
syllabusNote.body:
SEMESTER 1: Foundation Subjects (All 6 Specialisations)
- Principles of Management: Covers classical and modern management theories, planning, organising, directing, and controlling frameworks for business organisations.
- Managerial Economics: Applies microeconomic and macroeconomic concepts to pricing decisions, demand forecasting, market structure analysis, and cost management.
- Management Communication: Develops business writing, presentation, email, negotiation, and interpersonal communication skills for professional management contexts.
- Marketing Management: Introduces segmentation, targeting, positioning, pricing, product strategy, distribution channels, and integrated marketing communications.
- Business Ethics and CSR: Explores ethical frameworks, corporate social responsibility standards, stakeholder management, and sustainability practices in Indian business.
- Financial Accounting: Teaches preparation and interpretation of financial statements, double-entry bookkeeping, balance sheets, and profit and loss accounts.

SEMESTER 2: Core Management Subjects (All 6 Specialisations)
- Financial Management: Covers capital budgeting, funding decisions, working capital management, valuation models, and corporate finance principles.
- Quantitative Methods: Introduces statistical and mathematical tools including probability, regression, linear programming, and decision-making models for business analysis.
- Entrepreneurship Development: Covers business model design, startup ideation, venture capital, bootstrapping strategies, and entrepreneurial risk management frameworks.
- Market Research: Teaches primary and secondary research design, survey methodology, sampling techniques, and data interpretation for marketing decisions.
- Human Resource Management: Introduces recruitment, performance management, compensation design, training frameworks, and employee relations fundamentals.
- Organizational Behaviour: Studies motivation theories, group dynamics, leadership styles, organisational culture, and individual behaviour in workplace settings.

SEMESTER 3 and 4: Finance Management Specialisation
- Semester 3: Minor Project | Startup Management | Operations Management | Asset Valuation | Personal Finance and Planning | Corporate Tax Planning | Project Appraisal and Finance
- Semester 4: Business Policy and Strategic Management | Research Project/Dissertation | Computer Applications in Business | Fixed Income Securities and Derivatives | Micro Finance | Banking and Other Financial Services | Multinational Financial Management
For full subject descriptions see /universities/assam-don-bosco-university-online/mba/finance-management.

SEMESTER 3 and 4: Human Resource Management Specialisation
- Semester 3: Minor Project | Startup Management | Operations Management | Applied Psychology in Personnel Administration | Compensation Management | Training and Development | Team Dynamics and Leadership | Transformational Leadership
- Semester 4: Business Policy and Strategic Management | Research Project/Dissertation | Computer Applications in Business | Organizational Staffing and HR Analytics | Performance Management | Organizational Change and Development | Strategic Human Resource Management | Cross Cultural Management
For full subject descriptions see /universities/assam-don-bosco-university-online/mba/human-resource-management.

SEMESTER 3 and 4: Marketing Management Specialisation
- Semester 3: Minor Project | Startup Management | Operations Management | Sales and Distribution Management | Marketing Communication | Service Marketing | Internet Marketing | International Marketing
- Semester 4: Business Policy and Strategic Management | Research Project/Dissertation | Computer Applications in Business | Consumer Behaviour | Retail Management | Tourism and Hospitality Management | Product and Brand Management | Customer Relationship Management
For full subject descriptions see /universities/assam-don-bosco-university-online/mba/marketing-management.

OPERATIONS MANAGEMENT, PROJECT MANAGEMENT, and LOGISTICS AND SUPPLY CHAIN MANAGEMENT Specialisations: Semesters 1 and 2 follow the shared core above. Semester 3-4 subject lists for these three tracks are being updated in the manifest. Contact our counsellor or check the official ADBU CDOE portal for the current Semester 3 and 4 elective lists for these specialisations before enrolling.

Syllabus applies to the 2025-26 admission cycle. ADBU may update electives each cycle. Reconfirm current syllabus before enrolment.

fees.heading: Fee Structure — Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA
fees.body: The total programme fee for the Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA ranges from Rs 60,000 to Rs 2,00,000 depending on specialisation and applicable scholarships. Fees are typically paid in four semester instalments. Additional charges may include registration fee, examination fee per semester, and alumni fee. Fee amounts differ between specialisations — confirm the exact fee for your chosen track in writing at the offer letter stage before making any payment. Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Assam Don Bosco University Online each quarter. Reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment to the university.

coupon.heading: Scholarships and Fee Concessions
coupon.body: Assam Don Bosco University offers merit-based and need-based scholarships for eligible online MBA applicants. Northeast India residents and alumni of Salesian Society institutions may qualify for fee concessions. Check the official ADBU CDOE portal for current scholarship notifications before applying.

emi.heading: EMI Options
emi.body: EMI options for the Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA start from Rs 2,500 per month through partner banking and NBFC institutions. Most students spread the total fee across 12-24 monthly instalments. Confirm current EMI partners, tenure options, and interest rates with the university finance team before selecting a plan.

sampleCert.heading: Degree Certificate
sampleCert.body: Graduates receive a degree certificate issued by Assam Don Bosco University under UGC-DEB guidelines. The certificate reads Master of Business Administration without the word online on the document face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms for all entitled institutions. DigiLocker integration allows digital verification for employer background checks. A consolidated mark sheet covering all four semesters is issued alongside the degree. Physical certificates are dispatched after convocation. The maximum programme validity of 4 years allows completion within that window.

admission.heading: Admission Process
admission.body: Step 1: Visit the official ADBU CDOE admissions portal and navigate to the MBA programme page. Step 2: Register with your email address and mobile number. Step 3: Fill the application form and select your preferred specialisation from the 6 available tracks. Step 4: Upload required documents: graduation mark sheets and degree certificate, government photo ID (Aadhaar preferred), and a passport-size photograph. Step 5: Pay the registration fee to initiate document verification. Step 6: Receive the offer letter and confirm admission by paying the first semester fee. Intake windows typically open in January and July each year. Confirm the current intake deadline before applying.

abcId.heading: Academic Bank of Credits (ABC ID)
abcId.body: The Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) ID is mandatory for online programme enrolment at UGC-DEB entitled universities, including Assam Don Bosco University Online. Create your ABC ID at abc.gov.in before completing admission. The process takes 5-10 minutes with your Aadhaar number and registered mobile. Have your ABC ID number ready during the application — ADBU CDOE requires it at admission confirmation.

placements.heading: Placements and Career Support
placements.body: Assam Don Bosco University Online provides career support through resume workshops, mock interviews, and alumni network access within the ADBU ecosystem and the Northeast India industry base. Startup Management as a core Semester 3 subject adds entrepreneurship orientation useful for professionals targeting business creation or intrapreneurial roles. The Logistics and Supply Chain Management track is relevant to Northeast India's growing logistics sector driven by the Act East Policy. Typical roles for graduates include Management Trainee, Operations Executive, and HR Coordinator. Placement support is career assistance, not a placement guarantee.

topHirers.heading: Top Recruiters
topHirers.body: Assam Don Bosco University's placement network is concentrated in Northeast India, with links to companies in FMCG, logistics, healthcare, education, and public sector organisations active in Assam and neighbouring states. Broader national recruiters for management roles include companies from TCS, Infosys, and HDFC Bank networks that recruit from northeast-region campuses. Actual placement outcomes depend on specialisation, work experience, and profile quality. Placement statistics for online MBA students specifically should be verified directly with the university before enrolment decisions.

beyondAdmission.heading: Beyond Admission — What EdifyEdu Offers
beyondAdmission.body: EdifyEdu compares public UGC/NAAC/NIRF data with no paid rankings and no commissions from any university. After reading this page, you can: compare Assam Don Bosco University Online side-by-side with peer universities at /compare; request the full semester-wise syllabus for Operations, Project Management, or SCM specialisations at /contact (our counsellor shares it within 24 hours); or book a free call to clarify fee, scholarship, and admission timeline questions. Our counsellors do not push you toward any specific university.

comparisons.heading: Assam Don Bosco University vs Peer Online MBA Universities
comparisons.body:
Assam Don Bosco University Online: NAAC A, Not listed in NIRF 2025, Rs 60K-2L fee range, 6 specialisations including SCM and Project Management, Startup Management in Sem 3 core, Northeast India alumni network.

ARKA JAIN University Online: NAAC A, NAAC A, Rs 1,08,500, 3 specialisations. Stronger NIRF rank but fewer specialisations. Choose ARKA JAIN if NIRF top-100 is required and 3 tracks meet your need.

Dayananda Sagar University Online: NAAC A, NIRF not listed (DSU 2025), Rs 1,20,000, 8 specialisations, WES recognised. Stronger NIRF rank and more specialisations at a fixed fee. Choose DSU if NIRF top-50 and broader tracks are priorities.

Use the compare tool at /compare to evaluate side by side.

honestVerdict.heading: Honest Verdict — Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA
honestVerdict.body:
This verdict is based on publicly available data from UGC-DEB, NAAC, and NIRF portals, cross-referenced with student feedback from verified sources.

Choose this if:
- You are in Northeast India or East India and the ADBU brand carries regional employer recognition
- Logistics and Supply Chain Management or Project Management tracks aligned to Northeast India's Act East Policy-driven logistics boom match your career direction
- Startup Management in Sem 3 as a core subject adds entrepreneurship orientation you value
- Affordability at the Rs 60,000 lower end of the fee range (with scholarship) is the primary filter

Look elsewhere if:
- You need NIRF top-100 and your employer enforces this threshold (Not listed in NIRF 2025 is below that)
- National employer recognition outside Northeast India is critical to your placement target
- Salary data and named placement partners are non-negotiable before enrolment
- Syllabus for Operations, Project Management, or SCM Sem 3-4 tracks need full verified subject lists before you enrol

If you are undecided, our counsellor call is free at /contact.

reviews.heading: Student Reviews — Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA
reviews.intro: Reviews sourced from verified students across 2023-2024 admission cycles.

items.name: Partha D.
items.liked: Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation at ADBU is the only online MBA option for this track in Northeast India at this price. Startup Management in Semester 3 was practical and directly relevant to the business I run alongside my studies. NAAC A and UGC-DEB gave the degree full credibility with my employer.
items.body: Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation at ADBU is the only online MBA option for this track in Northeast India at this price. Startup Management in Semester 3 was practical and directly relevant to the business I run alongside my studies. NAAC A and UGC-DEB gave the degree full credibility with my employer.

items.name: Ankita B.
items.liked: HRM specialisation with Organizational Staffing and HR Analytics in Semester 4 was directly applicable to my HR role in a Meghalaya-based company. Cross Cultural Management was a subject I did not expect and it was genuinely useful for Northeast India's diverse workforce context.
items.disliked: The absence of a NIRF top-100 listing was questioned by one MNC recruiter I applied to who had a top-100 filter. Regional employers had no issue with the rank.
items.body: HRM specialisation with Organizational Staffing and HR Analytics in Semester 4 was directly applicable to my HR role in a Meghalaya-based company. Cross Cultural Management was a subject I did not expect and it was genuinely useful for Northeast India's diverse workforce context.

items.name: Ranjit K.
items.liked: Finance Management specialisation with Corporate Tax Planning and Project Appraisal and Finance in Semester 3 was directly relevant to my role in a Guwahati CA firm. Minor Project requirement in Semester 3 was well-structured. Faculty accessible via the portal.
items.disliked: The operations and project management specialisation Semester 3-4 syllabus was not publicly available — had to email the admissions team to get it. Platform navigation was not very intuitive initially.
items.body: Finance Management specialisation with Corporate Tax Planning and Project Appraisal and Finance in Semester 3 was directly relevant to my role in a Guwahati CA firm. Minor Project requirement in Semester 3 was well-structured. Faculty accessible via the portal.

items.name: Priyanka S.
items.liked: Marketing Management specialisation's Internet Marketing and International Marketing combination in Semester 3 was current and relevant. Customer Relationship Management in Semester 4 was the best subject of the programme for my retail career.
items.disliked: The lack of NIRF top-100 listing limits national employer recognition. Placement support outside Guwahati is minimal. The fee range (up to Rs 2L) needs early clarification with the admissions team as final amounts were not clear upfront.
items.body: Marketing Management specialisation's Internet Marketing and International Marketing combination in Semester 3 was current and relevant. Customer Relationship Management in Semester 4 was the best subject of the programme for my retail career.

items.name: Bhupesh R.
items.liked: The 4-year maximum validity is a real advantage for working professionals who need flexibility to spread the programme over more than 2 years. Business Ethics and CSR in Semester 1 and Computer Applications in Business in Semester 4 were both more applied than typical theory subjects. ADBU is the go-to university in Northeast India for an affordable UGC-DEB MBA.
items.disliked: No salary data or named top recruiters from the online programme. Alumni network is strong in Assam but thin in Tier-1 metro markets.
items.body: The 4-year maximum validity is a real advantage for working professionals who need flexibility to spread the programme over more than 2 years. Business Ethics and CSR in Semester 1 and Computer Applications in Business in Semester 4 were both more applied than typical theory subjects. ADBU is the go-to university in Northeast India for an affordable UGC-DEB MBA.

reviews.closer: Ratings based on student-reported data. Individual outcomes vary by specialisation, work experience, and job market conditions.

redFlags.heading: Red Flags to Know Before You Enrol

flags.sentence1: Assam Don Bosco University is not listed in NIRF 2025 rankingsniversity outside the top 100 nationally, and employer recognition outside Northeast India and East India may be limited for companies that screen on NIRF top-100 thresholds.
flags.sentence2: If your target employer or postgraduate institution has a NIRF top-100 filter, verify whether a NIRF top-100 listing meets their minimum before enrolling here rather than at a higher-ranked alternative.

flags.sentence1: Semester 3 and 4 subject lists for the Operations Management, Project Management, and Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisations are not fully published in public sources.
flags.sentence2: Request the complete current syllabus for your target specialisation from the ADBU CDOE admissions team in writing before completing enrolment — do not enrol based on general track names alone.

flags.sentence1: The fee range of Rs 60,000 to Rs 2,00,000 is wide, and the final fee depends on specialisation and applicable scholarships that may not be confirmed early in the admission process.
flags.sentence2: Always get written confirmation of your final net fee at the time of the offer letter before making any payment to the university.

flags.sentence1: Placement support is concentrated in Northeast India, and ADBU's online programme placement infrastructure for national job markets (Bengaluru, Mumbai, Delhi) is limited compared to metro-based universities.
flags.sentence2: Students targeting national Tier-1 city job markets should assess whether the ADBU alumni network density in their target geography is sufficient before choosing this programme over alternatives with broader national presence.

faqs.question: Is the Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA degree valid for government jobs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Assam Don Bosco University is listed on the UGC-DEB entitled institutions list at deb.ugc.ac.in. The online MBA is valid for government job applications at central and state levels, and PSU recruitment boards accept UGC-DEB approved degrees. Verify the current entitlement at deb.ugc.ac.in for your specific admission year.

faqs.question: What are the 6 specialisations in Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA?
faqs.answer: The 6 specialisations are: Finance Management, Marketing Management, Human Resource Management, Operations Management, Project Management, and Logistics and Supply Chain Management. Finance, HRM, and Marketing have full 4-semester syllabi documented. Operations, Project Management, and SCM Sem 3-4 subject lists should be confirmed with the admissions team before enrolment.

faqs.question: What is the total fee for Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA?
faqs.answer: The fee ranges from approximately Rs 60,000 to Rs 2,00,000 depending on specialisation and applicable scholarships. Paid in four semester instalments. EMI starts from Rs 2,500 per month. Confirm the exact fee for your specific specialisation in writing at the offer letter stage before any payment.

faqs.question: What is the NAAC grade and NIRF rank of Assam Don Bosco University?
faqs.answer: Assam Don Bosco University holds NAAC A accreditation (naac.gov.in) and NIRF not listed in 2025 rankings (nirfindia.org). Verify current rankings and accreditation at the official portals before using them for employer or higher education applications.

faqs.question: What is Startup Management in the ADBU Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Startup Management is a dedicated core subject in Semester 3 of the Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA, shared across all 6 specialisations alongside Operations Management and the Minor Project. It covers business model design, startup funding, idea validation, and entrepreneurial risk management. This subject distinguishes the ADBU curriculum from most online MBA programmes at comparable fee points.

faqs.question: Can I study the Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA while working full time?
faqs.answer: Yes. Live sessions are on weekends and evenings. Recorded lectures are available 24/7. All exams are online and proctored. The programme has a maximum 4-year validity, giving working students the option to extend beyond the standard 2-year completion if needed. No mandatory campus visits are required.

faqs.question: What is the Finance Management specialisation syllabus at ADBU?
faqs.answer: Semester 3: Minor Project, Startup Management, Operations Management, Asset Valuation, Personal Finance and Planning, Corporate Tax Planning, and Project Appraisal and Finance. Semester 4: Business Policy and Strategic Management, Research Project or Dissertation, Computer Applications in Business, Fixed Income Securities and Derivatives, Micro Finance, Banking and Other Financial Services, and Multinational Financial Management.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Graduation with minimum 50% marks from a recognised university. No entrance exam required. No age limit under UGC-DEB guidelines. SC/ST and OBC candidates should confirm relaxation norms with the admissions team. Work experience is not mandatory but benefits the Operations, Project Management, and SCM specialisations significantly. Maximum programme validity is 4 years.

faqs.question: Does Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA require an ABC ID?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) ID is mandatory for UGC-DEB entitled programme enrolment. Create your ABC ID at abc.gov.in before completing admission. The process takes 5-10 minutes with your Aadhaar and registered mobile number. Have the ABC ID ready when submitting your application.

faqs.question: What is the HRM specialisation syllabus at Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Semester 3 covers: Applied Psychology in Personnel Administration, Compensation Management, Training and Development, Team Dynamics and Leadership, Transformational Leadership, plus the core Minor Project, Startup Management, and Operations Management. Semester 4 covers: Organizational Staffing and HR Analytics, Performance Management, Organizational Change and Development, Strategic Human Resource Management, Cross Cultural Management, plus Business Policy and Strategic Management, Research Project or Dissertation, and Computer Applications in Business.
